paper_metadata.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
paper_metadata.abstract: While the Transformer architecture has become the de-facto standard for natural language processing tasks, its applications to computer vision remain limited. In vision, attention is either applied in conjunction with convolutional networks, or used to replace certain components of convolutional networks while keeping their overall structure in place. We show that this reliance on CNNs is not necessary and a pure transformer applied directly to sequences of image patches can perform very well on image classification tasks. When pre-trained on large amounts of data and transferred to multiple mid-sized or small image recognition benchmarks (ImageNet, CIFAR-100, VTAB, etc.), Vision Transformer (ViT) attains excellent results compared to state-of-the-art convolutional networks while requiring substantially fewer computational resources to train.

extraction_data.innovation: Hybrid model combining ResNet feature maps with Vision Transformer, enabling improved performance on smaller datasets by leveraging convolutional inductive biases.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning for computer vision tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Image recognition at scale using hybrid CNN-Transformer architectures
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Image classification via hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream image classification tasks
extraction_data.extension: Hybrid architecture feeds intermediate feature maps from ResNet stages into ViT with a patch size of one 'pixel' to enable seamless integration of CNN and Transformer components.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Vision Transformer (ViT) applies a pure Transformer encoder architecture directly to image patches, eliminating the need for convolutional layers and enabling scalable image recognition with fewer inductive biases for vision tasks.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning for computer vision tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Image recognition at scale using Transformer architectures
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream image classification tasks
extraction_data.extension: Patch embedding mechanism divides images into fixed-size patches (e.g., 16x16 pixels) and linearly embeds them as input tokens for the Transformer.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Vision Transformer (ViT) with larger patch size (32x32) to reduce sequence length and computational cost, enabling efficient training on smaller datasets.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning for computer vision tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Image recognition at scale using Transformer architectures
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream image classification tasks
extraction_data.extension: Patch embedding mechanism divides images into fixed-size patches (e.g., 32x32 pixels) and linearly embeds them as input tokens for the Transformer.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Larger Vision Transformer variant with increased model capacity, demonstrating improved performance on large-scale datasets while maintaining the same patch-based Transformer architecture.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning for computer vision tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Image recognition at scale using Transformer architectures
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream image classification tasks
extraction_data.extension: Patch embedding mechanism divides images into fixed-size patches (e.g., 16x16 pixels) and linearly embeds them as input tokens for the Transformer.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Larger Vision Transformer variant with larger patch size (32x32) to balance model capacity and computational efficiency, enabling scalable training on diverse datasets.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning for computer vision tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Image recognition at scale using Transformer architectures
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream image classification tasks
extraction_data.extension: Patch embedding mechanism divides images into fixed-size patches (e.g., 32x32 pixels) and linearly embeds them as input tokens for the Transformer.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Largest Vision Transformer variant introduced in the paper, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance on challenging image classification benchmarks by leveraging increased model capacity and large-scale pretraining.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning for computer vision tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Image recognition at scale using Transformer architectures
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream image classification tasks
extraction_data.extension: Patch embedding mechanism divides images into fixed-size patches (e.g., 14x14 pixels) and linearly embeds them as input tokens for the Transformer.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Axial Vision Transformer (Axial-ViT) replaces global self-attention with axial self-attention (row and column attention) to improve computational efficiency while maintaining performance.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient image recognition using Transformer architectures
extraction_data.extension: Axial self-attention enables efficient processing of 2D image inputs by decomposing attention into row and column operations, augmented by relative positional encoding.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Axial Vision Transformer (Axial-ViT) with finer patch resolution (16x16) for improved performance on image recognition tasks using axial self-attention.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient image recognition using Transformer architectures
extraction_data.extension: Axial self-attention enables efficient processing of 2D image inputs by decomposing attention into row and column operations, augmented by relative positional encoding.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
